title: Germany, KNDS Family Owners Agree on Stake Sale Ahead of IPO
summary: Families that own half of tankmaker KNDS NV agreed to sell a 40% stake to the German government, according to people familiar with the matter, paving the way for one of Europe’s key defense companies to go public.

title: CRH Near Biggest-Ever Deal For Arcosa, Financial Times Says
summary: Building materials group CRH Plc. is close to its biggest deal ever to acquire competitor Arcosa Inc., and an agreement could come as soon as next week, the Financial Times reports, citing people familiar with the matter.

title: Prediction Market Kalshi Eyes IPO as Revenue Hits $2 Billion
summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Prediction Market Kalshi Eyes IPO as Revenue Hits $2 Billion 
Prediction market giant Kalshi is exploring a potential IPO for 2027 or 2028 after annualized revenue surpassed $2 billion.

title: Inception Labs' Mercury 2 AI Beats Google's DiffusionGemma at Its Own Game
summary: Both models trade word-by-word generation for parallel denoising. Only one of them does it without losing intelligence in the trade.

title: AI 'Amplification Spiral' May Be Causing Delusions Among Users, Study Suggests
summary: New research outlines how chatbot behaviors, including personalization, mirroring, and excessive agreement, reinforce delusions.

title: Fentanyl-linked Chinese network tied to crypto fraud from Japan involving fake ‘Zksync.jp’ token: Nikkei
summary: The linked criminal group allegedly distributed a scam token under the name "zksync.jp" to deceive crypto users worldwide, with losses of over $1 million.

title: Notorious ‘jaredfromsubway’ MEV bot drained for roughly $7.5 million in counter-MEV honeypot
summary: An X account using the bot's name claimed a $15 million loss and offered a $1 million bounty, but evidence shows it is a likely impersonator.

title: US and Iran hold crunch talks in Switzerland
summary: Negotiations to permanently end the war focus on Israeli-Hizbollah conflict in Lebanon

title: Why Giorgia Meloni turned on Donald Trump
summary: Italian premier’s fallout with US president comes as she gears up for re-election battle

title: ‘I’ll probably be working until I die’: I’m 60, work as a waiter and have $2,000 in a Roth IRA. What will happen to me?
summary: “I’m afraid I’m in a bad place.”

title: I’m spending $170,000 to upgrade my home for my aging parents. Can I get tax breaks?
summary: “Given that at least half of the remodeling cost is to accommodate my disabled mother, I’m wondering if some of this may be tax deductible.”
